name: Baby Salmon Stuffed with Caviar III (Assembly)
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and salt and pepper the baby salmon.
2. Pipe the salmon mousse into the inside of the salmon.
3. Add a line of caviar inside of the salmon, then sprinkle with chopped chives.
4. Close the fish.
5. In a sauté pan, heat white wine.
6. Add shallot puree and bay leaf to the white wine in the pan.
7. Place the fish (belly down) in the pan and bake at 350-400°F for 20 minutes.
8. Remove the fish from the pan, skin it, and set aside.
9. Heat the juices in the pan for a few minutes.
10. Strain the juices into a saucepan containing red wine butter sauce (see page 28).
11. For assembly, place the baby salmon on a serving dish.
12. Spoon some of the salmon-flavored red wine sauce over half of the fish.
13. Spoon the wine butter sauce (flavored with the poaching liquid) over the other half.
14. Garnish with cucumber, tomato, chopped chives, salmon eggs, and crayfish tails (boiled).
